title: Ship class document
description: A hull's multi-deck plan (deck-plan format v3, docs/deckplan-format.md) plus the cargo characteristics M3 trading needs. Derived from the Gleam decoders in server/src/dh_server/shipclass.gleam (ship_class_decoder/cargo_decoder/handling_decoder) and server/src/dh_server/deckplan.gleam (decoder, embedded at the top level via decode.then) — those decoders are the source of truth; this schema is documentation and a typo-catcher, not a substitute for them. additionalProperties is false at every object level even though the decoders themselves ignore unknown fields, precisely so this schema can catch typos the decoders would silently swallow.

consoles.description: Optional. Authored as center glyphs in the grids (h=helm, c=cargo, b=broker, Q=docking port) and derived at load; the server re-emits this list (namespaced, over the wire) but hand-authored docs omit it.

capacity.description: Hold size in cargo units. Must be >= 0 (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

handling.description: How cargo physically gets aboard: break-bulk hulls load by robot stevedores anywhere; container hulls need a station crane and never open their holds.

dock_port_orientation.description: Optional field (decode.optional_field, default pi/2). This hull's docking-port outward normal in its own frame (ship-local radians, 0 = nose/+x). pi/2 = port flank (side-on mooring, the default). Combined with a station berth's orientation to fix the moored heading (issue #14).

grid.description: 3*height rows of 3*width chars: each tile is a 3x3 block (centre = floor/void/stairs; N/E/S/W edge-mids = wall/door/fixture). See docs/deckplan-format.md. Row count and each row's length must be multiples of 3 and consistent (enforced by the Gleam decoder, not by this schema).

kind.description: Free-form in the decoder (e.g. "helm"/"cargo" are conventions, not constraints); left open here. At least one console of kind "helm" is required by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema.

deck.description: Which deck index this console sits on (default 0).
Which deck arriving characters appear on (default 0).

tile.description: [x, y] tile where arriving characters appear (the airlock/gangway end).
